info.name: Healthcare Booking API — v5.0 Final
info.description:
Complete production-ready API for healthcare appointment booking.

## Quick Start
1. Import this collection + create environment: Healthcare Local (baseUrl = http://localhost:3000)
2. Run requests top-to-bottom — pre-request scripts auto-save tokens and IDs
3. OTPs print to server console in dev mode

## Phases Delivered
- Phase 1: Auth (OTP), Patient profile
- Phase 2: Hospitals, Doctors, Schedules, Slots, Bookings, Payments (Razorpay)
- Phase 3: Queue management, Receptionist operations, Notifications, OPD Sessions
- Phase 4: Search & Discovery, Admin dashboard, Cron jobs
- Phase 5: Tests (29 passing), Swagger docs, SMS provider, bug fixes

## Swagger UI
http://localhost:3000/api/docs (dev mode)

## Test Credentials
| Mobile | Role |
|--------|------|
| 9000000001 | super_admin |
| 9000000002 | hospital_admin |
| 9000000003 | receptionist |
| 9000000010 | doctor (Orthopedics) |
| 9876543210 | patient (complete profile) |
| 9876543212 | patient (incomplete profile) |

item.name: 🔧 Setup
item.description: Utility requests. Run Health Check first to confirm the server is up.

request.description: Confirms the server, database, and Redis are connected.

item.name: 1️⃣ Auth
item.description:
OTP-based authentication. In development, OTPs are printed to the server console.

**Flow:** Request OTP → copy from console → Verify OTP → tokens saved automatically.

request.description:
Sends OTP to mobile. In dev mode, OTP appears in server console logs.

Rate limited: 5 requests per 15 minutes per IP.

Change mobile to test different users:
- 9876543210 (complete profile)
- 9876543212 (incomplete profile)
- 9000000001 (super admin)
- 9000000002 (hospital admin)

exec: console.log('Check server console for OTP value (dev mode)');

request.description:
Verifies OTP and returns access + refresh tokens.

Tokens are automatically saved to collection variables:
- accessToken
- refreshToken
- profileStatus
- userId

profile_status will be 'incomplete' for new users and 9876543212.

exec: console.log('⚠️  Profile incomplete — run Complete Profile before booking');

request.description: Issues a new access + refresh token pair. Old refresh token is invalidated (rotation).
Blacklists the current access token JTI in Redis and invalidates the refresh token. Subsequent requests with the same token will return 401.
Tests the invalid OTP error response. Expects 401 with AUTH_OTP_INVALID code.
Tests Zod validation — invalid mobile format and non-numeric OTP.

item.name: 2️⃣ Patient Profile
item.description: Patient profile management. Login as a patient first (mobile: 9876543210 for complete, 9876543212 for incomplete).

request.description: Returns own profile including completion percentage and missing_fields array.

Fills the required fields for a new user. Returns new tokens with profile_status: 'complete'.

Use this after logging in as 9876543212 (incomplete profile).

exec: // Save new tokens — these have profile_status: 'complete' in JWT

request.description: Partial update — send only fields you want to change. Requires complete profile.
Login as 9876543212 (incomplete profile) first. This should return 403 PROFILE_INCOMPLETE with missing_fields and completion_url.

exec: // Only test this if profile is incomplete
console.log('Profile is complete — test with an incomplete user (9876543212)');

item.name: 3️⃣ Hospitals
item.description:
Hospital management. Register and manage hospitals.

**Login as super admin (9000000001) for POST/PATCH endpoints.**

request.description: Public endpoint — lists all live hospitals. Filter by city.
Returns full hospital details including staff list.

Creates a new hospital and links the admin user. Requires super_admin role.

Login as 9000000001 before running this.

Moves hospital through onboarding stages. Valid statuses:
registered → documents_pending → documents_submitted → verified → agreement_pending → agreement_signed → setup_in_progress → live

Setting to 'live' also sets went_live_at and is_verified = true.
Adds a receptionist to the hospital. Creates user if mobile doesn't exist. Login as hospital admin (9000000002).

item.name: 4️⃣ Doctors
item.description:
Doctor management and verification.

**Login as hospital admin (9000000002) for POST, super admin (9000000001) for PATCH verify.**

request.description: Public endpoint — lists verified, active doctors. Results ordered by reliability_score DESC.

exec: console.log('No doctors found. Try removing filters or run Phase 2 seeder.');

request.description: Returns full doctor profile including all hospital affiliations.
Registers a new doctor and creates hospital affiliation. Doctor starts with verification_status: 'pending' — must be approved by super admin before appearing in search.

exec: console.log('Status:', body.data.verification_status, '— needs super admin approval');

request.description:
Approves a doctor after NMC verification. Doctor will now appear in search results.

Login as super admin (9000000001) before running.
Rejects a doctor — they will not appear in search.
Creates a weekly schedule entry for a doctor at a hospital. After creating schedules, run 'Generate Slots' to create actual bookable slot records.

item.name: 5️⃣ Schedules & Slots
item.description:
Slot generation and browsing.

**Flow:** Create Schedule → Generate Slots → Browse Slots → Book Appointment

request.description:
Generates bookable slot records from the weekly schedule template for the next N days.

Safe to run multiple times — uses INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.
Returns { generated: N, skipped: M } counts.

query.description: YYYY-MM-DD — change to a future weekday

request.description:
Public endpoint — returns all available slots for a doctor on a given date.

Change the date to a future weekday (Mon–Fri) that falls within the generated range.
Results are cached in Redis for 60 seconds.

exec: console.log('No slots. Either date has no schedule or all slots are booked.');

item.name: 6️⃣ Appointments
item.description:
Booking, viewing, and cancelling appointments.

**Prerequisites:**
1. Login as a patient with complete profile (9876543210)
2. Have a valid slotId from 'Get Available Slots'
3. Have doctorProfileId and hospitalId saved

**The booking uses 3-layer race condition protection:**
- Redis distributed lock (SET NX EX 5)
- Sequelize SELECT FOR UPDATE
- UNIQUE constraint on slot_id

request.description:
Books an appointment. Requires:
- Complete patient profile (403 PROFILE_INCOMPLETE otherwise)
- Valid future slot with status: available
- Doctor affiliated with hospital

On success: returns appointment_id and consultation_fee breakdown.
Returns the authenticated patient's full appointment history, ordered by scheduled_at DESC.
Returns full appointment detail. Patients can only view their own appointments.

Cancels the appointment and frees the slot back to available.

Returns refund_eligible based on payment status and time until appointment.
Attempts to book an already-booked slot. Should return 409 SLOT_UNAVAILABLE. Run AFTER a successful booking with the same slotId.

item.name: 7️⃣ Payments
item.description:
Payment initiation, verification, and webhook handling.

**Flow:** Initiate → Razorpay Checkout (frontend) → Verify

In development, Razorpay key is a stub. The verify endpoint accepts any values but validates signature only if RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET is set.

request.description:
Creates a Razorpay order for the appointment. Returns order_id, amount, and key_id.

In production: pass order_id to Razorpay checkout on frontend.
In dev: stub order ID is returned.

Called after Razorpay checkout completes. Verifies HMAC-SHA256 signature, updates payment status to 'captured', and confirms the appointment.

Signature check is skipped if RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET is not set (dev mode).

Simulates Razorpay sending a payment.captured webhook.

Key idempotency behavior:
- First call: processes and returns 200
- Second call with same 'id': returns 200 immediately without re-processing (duplicate detected)

Amount is in paise (80000 = ₹800). The signature check is bypassed if RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET is not set.

exec: // Always 200 — even on failure — to prevent Razorpay retrying

request.description:
Sends the SAME event_id as the previous webhook request.

Should return 200 with 'Event already processed.' — confirming idempotency is working. The appointment should NOT be double-confirmed.

exec: console.log('✅ Idempotency working — duplicate event skipped');

item.name: 8️⃣ Edge Cases & Errors
item.description: Tests for authentication errors, rate limiting, and validation failures.

request.description: Calls a protected endpoint without Authorization header. Expects 401 AUTH_TOKEN_MISSING.
Sends a malformed JWT. Expects 401 AUTH_TOKEN_INVALID.
Login as a patient and try to register a hospital. Expects 403 AUTH_INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS — only super_admin can register hospitals.
Passes a non-UUID string as a doctor ID. Expects 400 VALIDATION_ERROR.
Requests a non-existent route. Expects 404 ROUTE_NOT_FOUND.
Sends an empty body to request-otp. Expects 400 VALIDATION_ERROR with details about missing mobile field.

item.name: 9️⃣ Queue
item.description:
Real-time queue position and wait time estimation.

**Flow:** Book appointment → appointment added to queue → Patient polls queue status → Receptionist calls next.

Queue position is cached in Redis (20s TTL). Estimates use rolling average consultation time and historical no-show rates.

request.description:
Returns patient's real-time queue position, estimated wait, and delay info.

Response includes:
- patients_ahead: effective count (accounts for no-shows)
- estimated_wait_minutes: includes break buffer every 60 patients
- cancel_refund_percentage: 0/50/100 based on delay duration
- doctor_status: available | in_consultation | delayed

Full day queue for a doctor — all patients in order.

Change date to today's date. Login as receptionist (9000000003).

item.name: 🔟 Receptionist
item.description:
Front-desk queue management. All routes require receptionist, hospital_admin, or super_admin role.

**Login as receptionist (9000000003) before running these.**

**Daily workflow:**
1. Doctor Check-In when doctor arrives
2. Mark Patient Arrived as patients come to clinic
3. Call Next to advance queue
4. Start Consultation when doctor begins
5. (repeat Call Next → Start Consultation)
6. Book Walk-In for unregistered patients

request.description: Marks doctor as arrived. Resolves any active delay event for today.

Reports doctor running late. Creates DoctorDelayEvent record.

This triggers:
- Queue estimates to increase by delay_minutes
- Affected patients shown 'cancel & refund' option if delay > 30 min
- Cache invalidation for this doctor's queue

Marks doctor as absent for the day.

This triggers:
- All today's PENDING and CONFIRMED appointments cancelled
- All captured payments marked as REFUND_PENDING
- Slots freed back to AVAILABLE

⚠️ This is irreversible for today's appointments.

Marks patient as physically present at the clinic. Sets arrived_at timestamp.

Required before the doctor can call the patient in.

Calls the next arrived patient.

Automatically:
- Completes the current in-progress consultation
- Updates rolling average consultation time on doctor profile
- Advances queue position
- Returns next patient's appointment_id

exec: if (d.queue_empty) console.log('✅ Queue is empty for today');

request.description:
Marks consultation as started. Sets actual_start_at timestamp.

Changes appointment status to IN_PROGRESS.
Marks patient as skipped (not present when token called). Shifts queue position count for others.

Books a walk-in patient directly. Creates user if mobile not found.

Walk-ins:
- Payment mode: cash (collected at reception)
- Appointment type: walk_in
- Added to queue with arrived_at set immediately
- 2% platform fee still calculated for reconciliation

item.name: 1️⃣1️⃣ Notifications
item.description:
Notification preferences and delivery history.

Notifications are processed asynchronously via BullMQ. In development mode they are logged to console instead of being sent.

Channels: SMS (MSG91), Push (FCM), Email (SES)

body.raw:
{
  "sms_enabled": true,
  "push_enabled": true,
  "email_enabled": false,
  "booking_reminders": true,
  "delay_alerts": true,
  "queue_position_alerts": true,
  "quiet_hours_enabled": true,
  "quiet_hours_start": "22:00",
  "quiet_hours_end": "07:00",
  "queue_notify_at_position": 2,
  "reminder_lead_time_hours": 2
}

request.description:
Updates notification preferences for the authenticated user.

Key settings:
- quiet_hours: notifications suppressed during this window (except critical)
- queue_notify_at_position: get alerted when this many patients are ahead
- reminder_lead_time_hours: appointment reminder sent N hours before

Full notification delivery history for the authenticated user.

Each record shows: channel, type, status, attempt_count, delivered_at.

item.name: 1️⃣2️⃣ OPD Sessions (High-Volume)
item.description:
Token-based OPD for high-volume doctors (100–500 patients/day).

**When to use:** Doctors with max_patients_per_day > 40 or where 20-min slot booking is impractical.

**Flow:**
1. Create Session (hospital admin)
2. Activate Session (receptionist, when doctor arrives)
3. Issue Walk-in Tokens at reception
4. Patients book Online Tokens via app
5. Call Next Token as doctor finishes each patient
6. Monitor via Session Stats

**Token issuance is atomic** (Redis lock prevents double-issuing same token number).

request.description:
Creates a token-based OPD session.

total_tokens = online_token_limit + walkin_token_limit

For a 4-hour session at 2 min/patient:
  4h × 30 patients/hr = 120 tokens max

Activates the session when doctor arrives and is ready to see patients.

Records actual_start_time. Session status: scheduled → active.

Issues a walk-in token. patient_mobile is optional — omit for anonymous walk-ins.

Walk-in tokens start with arrived status immediately.

Calls the next token.

- Marks current in-progress token as COMPLETED
- Updates session's rolling average consultation time
- Returns next token number
- When all patients seen, session auto-completes

Returns live session statistics.

Shows: current_token, tokens_issued, completed, waiting, skipped, estimated_end_time, avg_time_per_patient

item.name: 1️⃣3️⃣ Search & Discovery
item.description:
Patient-facing search. No auth required.

Features:
- Symptom-to-specialisation mapping (e.g. "knee pain" → orthopedics)
- Autocomplete with doctor names + specialisations
- Distance-based ranking when lat/lng provided
- Wilson score ratings (penalises low review count)
- Availability-weighted results

request.description:
Type-ahead suggestions. Returns doctor names AND matching specialisations.

Try:
- "knee" → maps to orthopedics
- "Dr. S" → doctor name match
- "cardio" → cardiology specialisation

Smart search maps symptoms to specialisations.

Response includes query_interpretation showing what was detected.
Filter by specialisation, fee cap, sort by reliability score.
Distance ranking via Haversine formula. Returns distance_km per doctor.

item.name: 1️⃣4️⃣ Admin
item.description:
Platform administration. Requires hospital_admin or super_admin role.

**Login as super admin (9000000001) for full access.**

request.description:
Live platform health snapshot.

Reads from Redis live counters (no DB aggregation).
Returns: bookings today, cancellations, live patients in queue, SMS delivery rate, payment success rate.

Active operational alerts requiring attention.

Alert types:
- doctor_delay: doctor running late today
- long_wait: patients waiting > 60 min
- notification_failures: messages that hit max retries
- pending_verification: hospitals awaiting document review > 2 days

Revenue breakdown.

period: today | week | month

Returns: GMV, platform_revenue (2%), doctor_payouts (98%), take_rate_pct, refund_count
Current month revenue summary.

Admin doctor list with filters.

verification_status: pending | approved | rejected
Suspends doctor account. They cannot log in until reactivated.
Reactivates a suspended doctor.

Manually triggers reliability score computation for all active verified doctors.

Normally run nightly by cron job. Useful after bulk data changes.

item.name: 1️⃣5️⃣ Docs & Utilities
item.description: API documentation and utility endpoints. No auth required.

request.description:
Opens the Swagger UI.

Only available in development mode or when SWAGGER_ENABLED=true.

You can also use this to download the raw OpenAPI spec:
  GET http://localhost:3000/api/docs.json
Raw OpenAPI 3.0 JSON spec. Import into Insomnia, Stoplight, or Postman.

Manually trigger slot generation for a doctor. Normally runs automatically at 2:30 AM IST via cron.

Login as hospital_admin (9000000002) before running.

Manually trigger a full search index rebuild. Normally runs at 2:35 AM IST.

Login as super_admin or hospital_admin.

Recompute reliability scores for all active doctors. Normally runs at 2:40 AM IST.

Login as super_admin.
